audit_status.source_base: Greek 2 Corinthians text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for letter of recommendation, tablets of hearts, new covenant, letter/Spirit, glory, veil, freedom, unveiled transformation. Anti-Jewish misuse warning included.

verses.restored: Are we beginning again to recommend ourselves? Or do we need, as some do, recommendation letters to you or from you?
You yourselves are our letter, having been written in our hearts, known and read by all humans,
being made manifest that you are a letter of Messiah, served by us, written not with ink but with Spirit of the living Divine, not on stone tablets but on tablets of human hearts.
Such confidence we have through Messiah toward the Divine.
Not that we are sufficient from ourselves to calculate anything as from ourselves, but our sufficiency is from the Divine,
who also made us sufficient as servants of a new covenant, not of letter but of Spirit; for the letter kills, but the Spirit makes alive.

But if the service of death, engraved in letters on stones, came in glory, so that the children of Israel were not able to gaze into the face of Moses because of the glory of his face, being made inactive,
how will not the service of the Spirit be in more glory?
For if the service of condemnation has glory, much more does the service of righteousness/justice abound in glory.
For indeed what had been glorified has not been glorified in this respect, because of the surpassing glory.
For if what was being made inactive came through glory, much more what remains is in glory.
Therefore, having such hope, we use much boldness,
and not as Moses would put a veil over his face so that the children of Israel might not gaze at the completion of what was being made inactive.
But their thoughts were hardened; for until this very day, at the reading of the old covenant, the same veil remains, not being unveiled, because in Messiah it is made inactive.
But until today, whenever Moses is read, a veil lies upon their heart.
But whenever one turns to the Lord, the veil is taken away.
Now the Lord is the Spirit; and where the Spirit of the Lord is, there is freedom.
But we all, with unveiled face, beholding/reflecting the glory of the Lord as in a mirror, are being transformed into the same image from glory into glory, just as from Lord, Spirit.

safety_note: 2 Corinthians 3 must not be used as anti-Jewish contempt. Paul is using Moses/veil/new covenant imagery to describe transformation in Messiah and Spirit, while still drawing from Israel's Scriptures. Handle the veil language as rhetorical and pastoral, not as a license to demean Jewish readers or Torah.
